steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject of the verb is "Mathematician Grigori Perelman" — singular. The phrase "sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S. Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau" is a parenthetical insertion (set off by commas) and does not change the subject.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a singular subject takes a singular verb; intervening parenthetical phrases don't affect agreement.
  • Choice A — "have been": plural verb form; does not agree with the singular subject "Perelman." ✗
  • Choice B — "are": plural verb form; does not agree with the singular subject "Perelman." ✗
  • Choice C — "is": singular verb form; correctly agrees with the singular subject "Perelman." ✓
  • Choice D — "are being": plural verb form; does not agree with the singular subject "Perelman." ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
